questions.question: What is the fundamental building block of sequential digital logic circuits, primarily used for storing a single bit of information?
options: AND gate
OR gate
Flip-flop
Multiplexer
Decoder

questions.question: In a computer system employing virtual memory, what is the primary purpose of a 'page table'?
options: To store the contents of the main memory for faster access
To manage the CPU's internal register file during context switching
To translate virtual addresses generated by the CPU into physical addresses in main memory
To define the instruction set architecture (ISA) for a given processor
To prioritize interrupts from various peripheral devices

questions.question: What is the primary role of 'parasitic extraction' in the backend of the digital IC design flow?
options: To convert the Register Transfer Level (RTL) code into a gate-level netlist
To verify the logical correctness of the circuit against its functional specification
To calculate the resistive and capacitive effects of interconnects and devices on the chip, crucial for accurate timing and power analysis
To generate manufacturing masks for the semiconductor fabrication process
To identify and correct any violations of design rules (DRC) in the physical layout

questions.question: In modern out-of-order execution processors, what is the fundamental principle behind 'Register Renaming'?
options: To allow software developers to name registers arbitrarily for improved code readability
To assign unique physical registers to architectural registers, thereby eliminating false data dependencies (WAR and WAW hazards)
To reorder instructions in the program sequence at compile time for better pipeline utilization
To dynamically adjust the number of available registers based on workload demands to save power
To create a hierarchical structure of registers within the CPU for faster access

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of a Harvard architecture over a Von Neumann architecture?
options: It uses a single address space for both instructions and data, simplifying memory management
It allows for simultaneous fetching of instructions and data, improving throughput
It provides inherent hardware-level security against malicious code injection
It is significantly cheaper to manufacture due to a simpler bus structure
It automatically caches frequently accessed data, reducing memory latency
